title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead a Patriots running back group that finished sixth in NFL rushing yards per game.
TreVeyon Henderson set a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry but struggled in postseason with 2.5 average.
The Patriots added fullback Reggie Gilliam and drafted Jam Miller, but depth at running back remains a concern.

title: Cyclist hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A cyclist was fatally struck by a vehicle in Mission Hill at 1457 Tremont St. around 8:20 a.m.
The driver stopped after the crash, and the Boston Police Department is investigating the incident.
Tremont Street was closed from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing until noon for the investigation.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Senate passed a bill targeting addictive social media features for minors with a 38-2 vote.
The bill requires disabling autoplay, infinite scroll, and algorithmic feeds for minors and limits late-night notifications.
The legislation focuses on protecting children's rights and privacy while maintaining access to social media platforms.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea officials seized a dog that attacked and seriously injured a smaller service dog on Chestnut Street.
The larger dog, determined unlicensed, is quarantined for 10 days as authorities investigate potential law violations.
The injured service dog, Snoopy Snoop Dog, will have its leg amputated due to the attack's severity.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones traded from the Patriots to Jacksonville for a sixth-round pick after a challenging tenure.
Jones discussed conflicts with Bill Belichick and the offensive decline post-offensive coordinator Josh McDaniels's departure.
Jones expressed relief after being traded, stating it was time to try something new in Jacksonville.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, advancing to the semifinals.
Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé scored the goals for France, overcoming a strong Moroccan defense.
Morocco's lineup changes included benching Ismael Saibari due to injury, while France made minimal adjustments.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: An altercation between journalists interrupted a France vs. Morocco press conference at Boston Stadium before the World Cup quarterfinal.
A reporter accused another of hitting him, leading to an intervention by FIFA and team officials to calm the situation.
Moroccan player Brahim Díaz humorously resumed the press conference, remarking he forgot the question after the incident.

title: After long search, scientists get amazing great white shark footage off R.I. coast
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Researchers captured footage of an eight-foot great white shark near a whale carcass southeast of Block Island.
Jon Dodd and Sarah Callan searched over 50 square miles of ocean before finding the whale and shark.
Rhode Island experienced a notable summer with several shark sightings, including porbeagle and thresher sharks.

title: Iconic Christmas Tree Shops signs are headed to auction
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Two 27-foot Christmas Tree Shops signs from Hyannis will be auctioned starting at $1,000.
Proceeds from the auction will benefit Habitat for Humanity of Cape Cod.
The former Christmas Tree Shops in Hyannis is being turned into a Best Buy, opening July 31.

title: A moment of appreciation for dominant Red Sox ace Sonny Gray
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray, Red Sox pitcher, holds a 10-1 record with a 2.61 ERA, yet was left off the All-Star team.
Since May 30, Gray has six straight quality starts and remains one of MLB's most consistent pitchers this season.
Despite potential trade interests, the Red Sox may rely on Gray as they enter the American League playoff race.
